engine.ladder_reasoning: A photographer whose portfolio (17 images, gallery pages, photo-heavy hero) IS the proof the visitor evaluates before booking; packages/prices support but do not replace the visual decision.

goal.one_action: book a photography session (or contact the photographer)

structure.archetype_note: Nav exposes 7 pages; 5 crawled (home, about, portfolio, contact, book). /contact and /book are client-rendered 'Laden...' shells so form/booking-widget content was not captured. Prices and Studio pages not crawled but package/pricing content appears inline on home.

mapped.observed_as: The Essence Session / The Signature Experience / The Full Experience / Business & Branding
Klaar Om Jouw Verhaal Te Vertellen?
About me (bio narrative, owner of Wolf Studios)

unmapped.why_no_fit: A full filterable gallery page is the core work-showcase surface but the section vocab has no dedicated 'portfolio/gallery' section id — 'socialProof' is the nearest but understates it as the primary proof surface.

confidence.why: fetch_quality is partial: /contact and /book render client-side 'Laden...' shells so the actual booking widget and contact form fields were not captured — the conversion mechanism (form vs embedded booking engine vs Calendly) is inferred, not observed. Design hints (fonts/colors) also empty.

gap_findings: A dedicated portfolio/gallery page (filterable, category-tabbed) is the primary proof surface for a work-engine site but has no canonical section id — it maps awkwardly onto socialProof.

Booking mechanism is client-rendered and uncaptured; cannot confirm whether it is a simple lead-form (in ICP) or an inventory/scheduling booking engine (would push toward in_icp:false). Classified in_icp:true on the assumption of a session-request form.

Packages/pricing tiers (€495/€1.495/€2.495 + no-price business tier) sit on a work-engine photography site — 'packages' capability covers it, but the mix of work-proof + explicit price ladder blends work and trust engines.
